description: Vulnerability disclosure programs, policies, reporting channels, and contacts — how to report security issues to an organization.
purpose: Track how to report vulnerabilities. Includes PSIRT programs, CNA (CVE Numbering Authority) roles and scopes, bug bounty platforms (HackerOne, Bugcrowd), security.txt files, and coordinated disclosure policies. Each program has a scope defining what it covers.
notes: The scope field on each disclosure entry answers 'if I found a vulnerability in X, does this program cover it?' Organizations may have multiple disclosure programs with different scopes — e.g., Red Hat has separate CNA, CNA-LR, and Root programs, each covering different categories of vulnerabilities. Contains all 502 CVE Program partners with their CNA scopes, contacts, and disclosure policies.
